sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Roma have struggled recently, with no wins in their last three matches, managing only two draws and a loss. Cagliari, on the other hand, are in strong form, securing three consecutive wins in their last five games, scoring impressively in recent outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been split with each team winning 1-0 at home. Roma will look to capitalize on playing at Stadio Olimpico, while Cagliari aim to repeat their recent upset from December.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Roma, sitting 5th with 43 points, boast a solid defense (least goals conceded in the league at 14) and strong possession play (58.17%). Cagliari, 12th with 28 points, have shown attacking intent recently but struggle defensively, conceding 31 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Roma’s Matías Soulé (6 goals, 4 assists) will be crucial, though they miss key players like Paulo Dybala. Cagliari rely on Sebastiano Esposito (3 goals, 4 assists), but injuries to players like Yerry Mina could weaken their backline.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Roma’s home advantage, defensive solidity, and Sofascore’s 58% home win probability, they are favorites. Cagliari’s recent form and attacking threat suggest they can challenge, but Roma’s superior league position and stats tilt the balance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half dominance by Roma
halftimeSections.text: Roma have taken control of the first half at the Stadio Olimpico with a well-deserved lead. They have dictated the pace of the game, showing superior ball possession and creating numerous opportunities in the final third. Their relentless pressure paid off with a goal from Donyell Malen in the 25th minute, capitalizing on a strong attacking move. Cagliari, on the other hand, struggled to gain a foothold, managing very limited entries into Roma's penalty area and failing to create any significant chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: Roma showcased consistent attacking intent throughout the first half, with multiple spells of sustained pressure in Cagliari's half. Their momentum peaked around the time of the goal, reflecting their ability to build up play effectively. While Cagliari had a couple of fleeting moments of forward movement, they were largely unable to maintain any rhythm or threaten Roma's defense, often being pushed back by the home side's dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers and missed opportunities
halftimeSections.text: Donyell Malen stood out for Roma, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat to Cagliari's backline. Players like Lorenzo Pellegrini and Gianluca Mancini also played crucial roles in creating chances, with key passes and assists driving Roma's attack. However, Roma missed a big chance, which could have seen them further ahead. Cagliari's lack of creativity was evident, with no big chances created and minimal impact in the attacking third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive solidity and discipline
halftimeSections.text: Roma's defense has been largely untested, with Cagliari unable to penetrate their backline effectively. The home side also dominated aerial duels, showcasing physical superiority. Cagliari, meanwhile, showed some resilience in defense despite the pressure but picked up a couple of yellow cards, indicating a struggle to contain Roma without conceding fouls. Their low percentage of duels won further highlights the physical mismatch in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Roma are well-positioned to extend their lead if they maintain their current intensity. Their ability to create chances and dominate possession suggests they could exploit Cagliari's defensive frailties further. Cagliari will need to adopt a more aggressive approach to turn the tide, but their lack of attacking threat so far makes an equalizer seem unlikely unless they can capitalize on a rare opportunity or a defensive lapse from Roma.
